topic: GitHub Agent HQ Guide 2026: Run Claude, Copilot, and Codex from One Interface

key_points: Official GitHub announcement: Claude and Codex now available in Agent HQ
No additional subscription required — included with Copilot Pro+ and Enterprise
Agents can commit code and comment on pull requests
Sessions can be started from github.com, GitHub Mobile, and VS Code
Each agent session consumes one premium request during public preview
Step-by-step setup guide for Agent HQ
Explains how to enable Claude and Codex in Copilot settings
Covers the Agents tab in GitHub repositories
Prerequisites: Copilot Pro+, Business, or Enterprise subscription
Organization-level policy configuration for enterprise control
Multi-agent development guide for running Claude, Codex, Copilot together
How to assign multiple agents to the same task to compare results
Workflow integration with pull requests and issues
One subscription unlocks Claude, Codex, Jules, Grok, Devin, and more
VS Code-focused Agent HQ guide
Agent session view inside VS Code editor
How to monitor agent progress in real time
Requires VS Code v1.109+ for cloud agent sessions
Practical tips for managing multiple AI agents without overwhelm
Best practices for choosing the right agent per task type
How to review and validate agent-generated PRs
Org-wide visibility and control of AI activity

stats.fact: GitHub Copilot reached approximately 20 million total users by July 2025
Copilot had 4.7 million paid subscribers as of January 2026, showing 75% year-over-year growth
GitHub Copilot holds 42% of the AI coding assistant market as of 2025
Copilot is deployed at approximately 90% of Fortune 100 companies
More than 50,000 organizations currently use GitHub Copilot
Claude and Codex entered public preview on GitHub on February 4, 2026
Availability expanded to Copilot Business and Pro users on February 26, 2026
GitHub Copilot generates 46% of code written by developers using it
Developers using Copilot report task speed-ups up to approximately 55%
AI code tools market projected to grow from $4.9B (2023) to $26B by 2030
Gartner forecasts 90% of enterprise software engineers will use AI coding assistants by 2028, up from under 14% in early 2024
Copilot Pro costs $10/month with 300 premium requests; Copilot Pro+ costs $39/month with 1,500 premium requests
Starting June 1, 2026, GitHub moves Copilot from request-based to usage-based billing with GitHub AI Credits

angles: One subscription, multiple frontier AI agents — no extra cost beyond existing Copilot plan
Run Claude, Codex, and Copilot simultaneously on the same task to compare approaches and reasoning
GitHub Agent HQ as enterprise command center: org-wide governance, audit trails, and access controls
GitHub Agentic Workflows: automating repetitive repository tasks (triage, docs, CI fixes) with AI agents
VS Code + GitHub integration: manage agent sessions without leaving your editor
Choosing the right agent for the right job: Claude for complex refactoring, Codex for autonomous delegation, Copilot for autocomplete
Agent HQ vs standalone tools like Cursor and Devin: pros of staying inside the GitHub ecosystem
Billing transition in 2026: understanding premium requests and the shift to AI Credits
